zh.Bridgnorth, Shropshire, England: 英國史洛普爾布利其諾斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Hubert de Saint Clair於大約1120年出生在英國時，他的父親Haimo de St Clair22歲，母親Gunnora of Essex26歲。他至少有1個女兒。他去世於1155年在英國史洛普爾布利其諾斯享年36歲。

zh-hans.Bridgnorth, Shropshire, England: 英国史洛普爾布利其諾斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hubert de Saint Clair于大约1120年出生在英国时，他的父亲Haimo de St Clair22岁，母亲Gunnora of Essex26岁。他至少有1个女儿。他去世于1155年在英国史洛普爾布利其諾斯享年36岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Luc, Camille, Lucien.  Spanish Alejandro, Anselmo, Carlos, Emilio, Jesusa, Jorge.
French (also Santé): probably a nickname for a healthy person, from French santé ‘good health’.

Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Spanish (southwestern): habitational name from one of several places called Sante, in Galicia, Portugal, and Asturias, from a Visigothic personal name. This surname is also found in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Clair
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: from the male personal name Clair (from Latin Clarus ‘illustrious’), which was borne by two early Christian saints, a 3rd-century bishop of Nantes and the 4th-century St. Clair of Aquitaine.
French: nickname for a cheerful individual, from the adjective clair ‘bright, light’. Compare Leclair 1.
American shortened form of French   Leclair   1, a cognate of 2 above.

variants.name: Leclair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Armand, Emile, Normand, Gaston, Adrien, Antoine, Lucien, Monique, Philias, Camille.

French: nickname for a cheerful individual, a variant of Clair 2 ‘bright, light’, with fused masculine definite article le. Derivation from the male personal name Clair 1 is also possible. Compare Laclair , Laclaire , and Leclear .
French: altered form of   Leclerc   1.

variants.name: Klar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Ezriel, Tzvi, Baruch, Devorah, Isadore, Nachman, Yitzchok.  German Gerhard, Johann, Erhard, Kurt, Willibald.
German: nickname from Middle High German and Middle Low German klār ‘pure, beautiful, clear’.
German: from the female personal name Klara, an equivalent of Clara (see Clair and Sinclair ).

variants.name: Cler
meanings: French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Clarin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Luz, Alejandro, Armida, Epifano, Florencio, Jose, Libia, Librada, Maria Luisa, Raul, Santos, Sergio.

Swedish: variant of Klarin, ornamental or perhaps habitational name formed with klar ‘clear’ (possibly representing an element of a placename) + the adjectival suffix -in, a derivative of Latin -in(i)us ‘relating to’.
French: from a pet form of the personal name   Clair  .

variants.name: Auclair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Marcel, Raoul, Andre, Fernand, Ovila, Alphonse, Cecile, Gaston, Germaine, Jacques, Jean-Paul.
French: patronymic from Clair , from (fils) au Clair ‘(son) of Clair’. Compare Leclerc 2 and Oclair .

History: Pierre and André Auclair, two brothers from Saint-Vivien near La Rochelle in France, are recorded in Charlesbourg, QC, in 1679 and 1681, respectively, the years of their marriage. The descendants of one or another brother also bear altered forms of the surname, such as Leclerc, Oclair, and O'Clair.

nameMeanings.name: Hubert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was popular among the Normans, who introduced it to Britain, where it was later reinforced by settlers from the Low Countries. An 8th-century St Hubert succeeded St Lambert as bishop of Maastricht and is regarded as the patron of hunters, since, like St Eustace , he is supposed to have seen a vision of Christ crucified between the antlers of a stag.

variants.name: Hupprecht
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was popular among the Normans, who introduced it to Britain, where it was later reinforced by settlers from the Low Countries. An 8th-century St Hubert succeeded St Lambert as bishop of Maastricht and is regarded as the patron of hunters, since, like St Eustace , he is supposed to have seen a vision of Christ crucified between the antlers of a stag.

variants.name: Huppert
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was popular among the Normans, who introduced it to Britain, where it was later reinforced by settlers from the Low Countries. An 8th-century St Hubert succeeded St Lambert as bishop of Maastricht and is regarded as the patron of hunters, since, like St Eustace , he is supposed to have seen a vision of Christ crucified between the antlers of a stag.

variants.name: Hugbert
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was popular among the Normans, who introduced it to Britain, where it was later reinforced by settlers from the Low Countries. An 8th-century St Hubert succeeded St Lambert as bishop of Maastricht and is regarded as the patron of hunters, since, like St Eustace , he is supposed to have seen a vision of Christ crucified between the antlers of a stag.

en.lifeSummary: When Hubert de Saint Clair was born about 1120, in England, his father, Haimo de St Clair, was 22 and his mother, Gunnora of Essex, was 26. He had at least 1 daughter. He died in 1155, in Bridgnorth, Shropshire, England, at the age of 36.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hubert de Saint Clair est né environ 1120, à Angleterre, son père, Haimo de St Clair, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Gunnora of Essex, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1155, à Bridgnorth, Shropshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 36 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hubert de Saint Clair um 1120 in England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Haimo de St Clair 22 und seine Mutter Gunnora of Essex 26. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1155 im Alter von 36 Jahren in Bridgnorth, Shropshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hubert de Saint Clair è nato il circa 1120 nel England, il suo padre, Haimo de St Clair, aveva 22 anni e il suo madre, Gunnora of Essex, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1155, nel Bridgnorth, Shropshire, England, all'età di 36 anni.

ja.Bridgnorth, Shropshire, England: イギリスシュロプシュアイアブリドグノース
ja.lifeSummary: Hubert de Saint Clairが約1120年にイギリスで生まれた時，父Haimo de St Clairは22歳，母Gunnora of Essexは26歳であった。彼は少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1155年にイギリスシュロプシュアイアブリドグノースで36歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hubert de Saint Clair(이)가 대략 1120년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Haimo de St Clair(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Gunnora of Essex(은)는 26세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1155년 영국 슈럽실르 브리지노르스에서 36세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hubert de Saint Clair nasceu aproximadamente 1120, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Haimo de St Clair, tinha 22 anos e sua mãe, Gunnora of Essex, tinha 26 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1155, em Bridgnorth, Shropshire, Inglaterra, com 36 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hubert de Saint Clair родился около 1120 г. в местности Англия, его отцу по имени Haimo de St Clair было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Gunnora of Essex, 26. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1155 г. в местности бриджнорт, шропшир, Англия в возрасте 36 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hubert de Saint Clair nació aproximadamente 1120, en Inglaterra, su papá, Haimo de St Clair, tenía 22 años y su mamá, Gunnora of Essex, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1155, en Bridgnorth, Shropshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 36 años.
